outlook.headline: 1.6T adoption is broadening, with momentum still building
outlook.summary: Mentions have risen to 0.8x, 22% above the trailing average, and every company read is bullish. The signal is moving from isolated hot demand to broader adoption and launch momentum, with tight supply/backlog at MACOM, Semtech and MaxLinear suggesting real near-term pull rather than just interest in the next upgrade cycle.

drivers.label: Broad-based demand momentum
drivers.detail: Multiple suppliers describe 1.6T demand as hot, strong or accelerating, indicating the product is still early in a fast ramp rather than entering digestion.

drivers.label: Tight supply and backlog support near-term upside
drivers.detail: Tight supply and backlog strength point to constrained availability and stronger shipment conversion over the next 1-2 quarters.

drivers.label: Adoption is moving into the mainstream of the upgrade cycle
drivers.detail: Broadcom frames 1.6T as the de facto standard, while Astera Labs sees it as part of the next Ethernet/PCIe upgrade cycle, supporting broader platform adoption.

drivers.label: Module adoption and telecom/datacenter activity are accelerating
drivers.detail: SiTime cites faster-than-expected optical module adoption, while Vishay points to rising telecom activity tied to 800G and 1.6T AI network switches.

risks: A pause in hyperscale deployment timing could turn hot demand into a shorter-lived burst.
Supply normalization could reduce the urgency/ASP tailwind for tight suppliers like MACOM, Semtech and MaxLinear.
If 1.6T remains concentrated in early adopters, mention momentum could outpace actual shipment breadth in the next quarter.
